mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 25 basis points over a 3-month rolling window, equity risk premiums compress as the cost of capital falls. This triggers positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 1.95% suggests late-cycle tightening environment
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when trigger is activated, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity. This mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 3.7% is near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 45% of activation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts simultaneously decline, signaling weakening aggregate demand. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST declines > 5% from 3-month average
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1282K units, RSAFS at 509B dollars
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 52% of joint-trigger instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines month-over-month, indicating supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors immediately reprice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 48% of observations, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market softening without immediate recession. This predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as growth expectations moderate.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5%, real rates are negative and constrained. This regime-specific mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as financial repression supports equity valuations.
regime_hint: financial repression with negative real rates
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-trigger instances, or the mechanism fails to outperform baseline by at least 2% annualized Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 2% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as terminal growth expectations reset lower and multiple compression occurs.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 48% of observations, or the mechanism produces positive returns in more than 50% of low-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rise above 1.3M annualized units while retail sales (RSAFS) simultaneously accelerate above 5% year-over-year, signaling robust demand momentum. This joint mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as cyclical growth expectations strengthen.
candidate_trigger: HOUST > 1.3M units AND RSAFS YoY growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 1282K, RSAFS at 509B dollars
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in fewer than 54% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
